sentiment.summary: Dow enters correction territory on fifth straight losing week as U.S.-Iran war blocks Strait of Hormuz, keeping oil near $100 and VIX spiking above 31.

insights.text: Dow in correction — fifth straight losing week and no bottom in sight. U.S.-Iran war has closed the Strait of Hormuz, locking in a $14-18 oil risk premium near $100. VIX above 31 screams fear. Energy is the only winner; everything else is bleeding. Do not fight this tape.

BTC ticking up to $66.6K but still 9% below your $73,135 cost basis. Weekend bounce (+0.44%) is noise vs. the macro trend. Geopolitical risk and risk-off flows keep crypto rangebound. No near-term catalyst to recover losses — hold, don't add.

RKLB drops to $60.93 — slipping below your $62.04 cost basis. $190M DoD hypersonics contract is a real fundamental win, but the broader selloff is overriding it. SpaceX IPO buzz sparked a brief pop that faded fast. 10 analysts target $90 avg — hold, stay patient.

SATS holds at $114.96 — right at your $114.33 cost basis with fresh tailwinds. S&P 500 inclusion triggered an 8% surge this week — structural index-fund buying is a real driver. Debt restructuring and Starlink strategic partnership add long-term upside. Hold and accumulate on dips.

TE at $5.98 — 11% below your $6.73 cost with earnings due March 31. Norway Statnett 50MW grid assignment shows operational momentum. Analysts avg $10.50 target. Beat earnings and this reverses hard — miss and it breaks lower. High-stakes binary event in 48 hours.

STX at $378 — down 16% from your $449 cost as AI disruption hits storage. Massive Q2 beat (EPS +11%, revenue +22% YoY, FCF +42% QoQ) crushed by Google TurboQuant AI compression triggering a sector-wide selloff. Fundamentals are strong — this is sentiment-driven pain.

TSAT near $36 — thin cushion above your $33.33 cost after the March 17 spike faded. Stock surged 19% to $42 on earnings then pulled back. Securities fraud investigations from multiple law firms add serious legal overhang. Lightspeed LEO is the only live catalyst — hold speculative.

BBAI at $3.13 — down 65% from your $9.02 cost with no floor visible. Revenue cratered 37.7% YoY, share dilution vote looms, and Cantor Fitzgerald slashed target to $5. Analysts see 120% upside on paper but execution is failing. Do NOT add — wait for evidence of a turn.

NBIS at ~$100 — 24% below your $131 cost but catalysts keep stacking up. Meta $27B AI infrastructure deal, Nvidia $2B investment, $4B convertible raise, and 1.2 GW Missouri campus approved. BofA targets $150. Dilution near-term, but long-term thesis is intact — conviction hold.

OKLO at $50.23 — down 60% from your $125 cost with no near-term floor. Regulatory hurdles and financing risks dominate. Avg analyst PT $101 implies 100% upside but commercialization timeline is highly uncertain. Nuclear thesis is real long-term — be patient, do not panic sell.

NVTS at $8.37 — 16% below your $10 cost but momentum is quietly building. New CFO Tonya Stevens starts March 30. APEC 2026 launches show product velocity. Q1 revenue guide of $8-8.5M beats estimates. Broader semis macro weakness is the primary drag — hold and monitor.
